title: The Five Levels of Claude
voiceScript: If you still use Claude like a chat box, you are stuck at Level one. Most people never climb. You burn 30 to 60 minutes a day re-explaining context and copying outputs between apps. Those are hours you never get back. There are five levels. Chat box, Projects, Co-work, Claude Code, then always on routines. Each one takes more off your plate. Here is the jump. Five levels, up to 60 minutes a day back, and 50 plus connectors you can wire in. At the top it stops being a tool. It becomes infrastructure that runs while you sleep. Which level are you on, and what task do you push up next? Follow for more.

scenes.voiceText: If you still use Claude like a chat box, you are stuck at Level one.

scenes.headline: The climb

scenes.voiceText: At the top it stops being a tool. It becomes infrastructure that runs while you sleep.
scenes.headline: It becomes *infrastructure*

scenes.voiceText: Which level are you on, and what task do you push up next? Follow for more.
scenes.cta: Which level are you on right now?

source: LinkedIn post + 'The Five Levels of Claude' breakdown. Progression from chat interface to autonomous system-level infrastructure. L1 Baseline: a search bar that returns paragraphs (~30 min/day saved, the floor). L2 Beginner: Projects (memory spine), 50+ connectors (Slack/Drive/Gmail/Notion), file creation, persistent artifacts, inline visuals, Office add-ins. L3 Intermediate (Co-work): read/write local file access in isolated VM, Skills (markdown workflows), /schedule, mobile Dispatch, Claude Design, computer use. L4 Advanced (Claude Code): terminal/CLI, claude.md, Plan Mode + sub-agents (Opus plan / Sonnet execute), git worktrees (parallel Claudes), MCP, verification loops. L5 Architect: cloud Routines (schedule/API/GitHub PR triggered), Hooks (pre-tool/post-edit/stop), channels + headless, memory consolidation (Autodream), agent teams + task budgets. Final barrier = trust; start with low-stakes deterministic automations.
